Ready to implement? Let's build this feature step by step!

docs.title: Stock Opname Librarian Work Completion Feature
Overview
docs.text: This feature allows administrators to manage and track librarian work completion status in stock opname sessions. Librarians can mark their work as completed, and administrators can view reports, manually complete work, or reopen completed sessions.

docs.title: 1. Librarian Work Completion with Confirmation
docs.text:
When librarians finish scanning items, they can mark their work as completed:

  • Confirmation Modal: Before completing work, librarians see a confirmation modal warning them that they won't be able to scan more items after completion.
  • Status Change: Once confirmed, their work status changes from "in_progress" to "completed".
  • Scan Prevention: Completed librarians cannot access the scanning interface unless an admin reopens their session.

docs.title: 2. Admin Librarian Reports Page
docs.text: A dedicated admin page (/manage/stock_opname/report) shows all sessions and librarian work status:

docs.title: Report Features:
docs.text:
• Session List: Displays all stock opname sessions with expandable details
  • Pagination: Sessions are paginated (10 per page) with previous/next navigation
  • Librarian Details: Shows each librarian's work status including:
  • Name/Email
  • Items checked count
  • Work status (pending/in_progress/completed)
  • Started and completed timestamps
  • Notes (if any)
  • Admin Operations:
  • Complete Work: Manually mark a librarian's work as completed
  • Reopen Work: Cancel completion and allow librarian to scan again

docs.title: 3. Scan Prevention After Completion
docs.text: When a librarian's work is completed: - They are automatically redirected from the scan page - A flash message informs them to contact an admin to reopen - The session must be reopened by an admin before they can scan again

docs.title: Database Schema
docs.text:
The stock_opname_librarian_assignments table tracks work status:

field :work_status, :string, default: "pending"
# Values: "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed"

field :items_checked, :integer, default: 0
field :started_at, :utc_datetime
field :completed_at, :utc_datetime
field :notes, :string

docs.title: StockOpname.complete_librarian_work/3
docs.text:
Marks a librarian's work as completed (called by the librarian).

StockOpname.complete_librarian_work(session, user, notes \\ nil)

Parameters: - session: The stock opname session struct - user: The librarian user struct - notes: Optional completion notes

Returns: {:ok, assignment} or {:error, reason}

docs.title: StockOpname.cancel_librarian_completion/2
docs.text:
Reopens a completed librarian session (admin only).

StockOpname.cancel_librarian_completion(session, user)

Parameters: - session: The stock opname session struct - user: The librarian user struct

Returns: {:ok, assignment} or {:error, reason}

docs.title: StockOpname.admin_complete_librarian_work/3
docs.text:
Manually completes a librarian's work (admin only).

StockOpname.admin_complete_librarian_work(session, user, notes \\ nil)

Parameters: - session: The stock opname session struct - user: The librarian user struct - notes: Optional completion notes

Returns: {:ok, assignment} or {:error, reason}

docs.title: StockOpname.get_session_librarian_report/1
docs.text:
Gets detailed work report for all librarians in a session.

StockOpname.get_session_librarian_report(session)

Parameters: - session: The stock opname session struct

Returns: List of maps containing: 

%{
  assignment: %LibrarianAssignment{},
  items_checked: integer(),
  user: %User{}
}

docs.title: Librarian Completing Work
docs.text:
1. Librarian finishes scanning items in a session
  2. Clicks "Complete Work" button on scan page
  3. Confirmation modal appears (using core components modal) with warning message
  4. Librarian can:
  5. Click "Cancel" or press ESC to dismiss
  6. Click outside modal to dismiss
  7. Click "Yes, Complete Work" to confirm
  8. If confirmed:
  9. Work status changes to "completed"
  10. completed_at timestamp is recorded
  11. Librarian is redirected to session show page
  12. If librarian tries to access scan page again:
  13. Redirected with error message
  14. Must contact admin to reopen session

docs.title: Admin Managing Completion
docs.text:
1. Admin navigates to /manage/stock_opname/report
  2. Views paginated list of sessions (10 per page)
  3. Uses pagination controls to navigate through sessions if needed
  4. Clicks on a session to expand librarian details
  5. For each librarian, admin can:
  6. If not completed: Click "Complete" to manually mark as done
  7. If completed: Click "Reopen" to cancel completion
  8. Actions are confirmed before execution
  9. Report updates in real-time after each action

docs.title: Routes
docs.text:
# Librarian scanning (checks completion status)
GET /manage/stock_opname/:id/scan

# Admin librarian reports
GET /manage/stock_opname/report

docs.title: Scan Page (scan.ex)
docs.text:
• confirm_complete_work - Completes the work and redirects

Note: The modal is controlled entirely by JS commands, no event handlers needed for show/hide.

Modal Implementation: 

<!-- Trigger Button -->
<button
  type="button"
  phx-click={show_modal("complete-work-modal")}
  class="px-4 sm:px-6 py-2 sm:py-3 bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 text-white font-medium rounded-lg transition-colors touch-manipulation"
>
  <.icon name="hero-check-circle" class="w-5 h-5 inline mr-1 sm:mr-2" /> Complete Work
</button>

<!-- Modal -->
<.modal
  id="complete-work-modal"
  show={false}
  on_cancel={hide_modal("complete-work-modal")}
>
  <!-- Modal content with cancel and confirm buttons -->
</.modal>

docs.title: Report Page (report.ex)
docs.text:
• toggle_session - Expands/collapses session details
  • complete_work - Admin manually completes librarian work
  • reopen_work - Admin reopens completed work

docs.title: Authorization
docs.text:
• Scan Page: Only assigned librarians or super admins can access
  • Report Page: Only super admins can access
  • Complete Work: Librarians can complete their own work
  • Reopen/Manual Complete: Only super admins can perform these actions

docs.title: Work Status Badge
docs.text: Displays librarian work status with appropriate colors: - Pending: Gray badge with clock icon - In Progress: Blue badge with arrow-path icon - Completed: Green badge with check-circle icon

docs.title: Confirmation Modal
docs.text: A modal dialog that: - Uses the <.modal> component from core_components.ex - Controlled entirely by JS commands (show_modal / hide_modal) - No LiveView assigns needed for visibility state - Warns about consequences of completion - Prevents accidental completion - Provides cancel option (using hide_modal JS command) - Uses accessible design patterns (ARIA labels, focus management) - Supports keyboard navigation (ESC to close, click-outside to dismiss)

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Always show confirmation before completing work
  2. Use JS-controlled modals for better performance (no LiveView round-trips)
  3. Check work status in mount to prevent access after completion
  4. Update reports in real-time after admin actions
  5. Log completion events for audit trail
  6. Provide clear feedback to users about completion status

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
Test scenarios to cover:

  1. Librarian completion flow:
  2. Can complete work when items are scanned
  3. Confirmation modal appears and works

  4. Cannot access scan page after completion

  5. Admin operations:

  6. Can view all sessions and librarians
  7. Can manually complete librarian work
  8. Can reopen completed work

  9. Reports update after actions

  10. Edge cases:

  11. Super admin scanning without assignment
  12. Librarian with no items checked
  13. Multiple sessions and librarians
  14. Concurrent completion attempts
  15. Pagination with many sessions

docs.text:
• Stock Opname Design
  • Stock Opname Quick Reference

docs.title: Future Enhancements
docs.text:

  • Completion statistics and analytics

docs.title: Creating a Session
docs.text:
1. Go to Dashboard → Stock Opname → New Session
  2. Fill in:
  3. Title and description
  4. Select nodes (libraries/branches)
  5. Choose collection types (book, journal, etc.)
  6. Select scope:
    • All: Check all items matching nodes and types
    • Collection: Check items in specific collection
    • Location: Check items in specific location
  7. Assign librarians who will scan items
  8. Review estimated item count
  9. Click Create Session

docs.title: Starting a Session
docs.text:
1. Go to Stock Opname → Select session → Start Session
  2. Librarians receive email notifications
  3. Status changes to "In Progress"

docs.title: Reviewing a Session
docs.text:
1. Wait for all librarians to complete their work
  2. Click Complete Session (triggers missing detection)
  3. Go to Review tab
  4. Review:
  5. Items with changes (before → after)
  6. Missing items
  7. Librarian work summary
  8. Choose action:
  9. Approve: Apply all changes
  10. Request Revision: Send back with notes
  11. Reject: Cancel without changes

docs.title: Scanning Items
docs.text:
1. Go to Stock Opname → Select assigned session → Scan Items
  2. Scan or type item identifier:
  3. Barcode
  4. Inventory code
  5. Item code
  6. Legacy item code
  7. If multiple matches appear, select the correct item
  8. Review item details
  9. Update if needed:
  10. Status (available, borrowed, lost, etc.)
  11. Condition (good, fair, poor, etc.)
  12. Availability (available, not available)
  13. Location
  14. Add notes
  15. Click Check Item
  16. Repeat for all items
  17. Click Complete My Work when done

docs.title: URL Paths
docs.text:
• List Sessions: /manage/stock-opname
  • New Session: /manage/stock-opname/new
  • Session Details: /manage/stock-opname/:id
  • Scan Items: /manage/stock-opname/:id/scan
  • Review: /manage/stock-opname/:id/review

docs.title: Session Statuses
docs.text: Status Description Actions Available Draft Just created, not started Start, Edit, Cancel In Progress Librarians scanning items Scan, Complete (admin) Completed Ready for review Review, Request Revision Pending Review Awaiting admin approval Approve, Reject, Request Revision Approved Changes applied View only Rejected Cancelled, no changes View only

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts (Scan Page)
docs.text:
• Enter after scanning: Automatically finds item
  • Tab: Navigate between fields
  • Esc: Clear current item

docs.title: For Admins
docs.text:
• Start Small: Begin with one location or collection
  • Assign Multiple Librarians: Distribute workload
  • Review Regularly: Check progress during scanning
  • Use Notes: Request revision with specific instructions
  • Export Results: Download reports for records

docs.title: For Librarians
docs.text:
• Use Barcode Scanner: Much faster than typing
  • Check Details: Verify item matches before checking
  • Update Conditions: Note any damage or issues
  • Add Notes: Document unusual situations
  • Take Breaks: Scanning can be tiring

docs.title: "Item Already Checked"
docs.text:
• Item was scanned by you or another librarian
  • Check recently scanned items list
  • If error, contact admin

docs.title: "Multiple Items Found"
docs.text:
• Common with items sharing barcodes
  • Select correct item from list
  • Use item code or inventory code for precision

docs.title: "Not Found"
docs.text:
• Item may not be in session scope
  • Check spelling of identifier
  • Verify item exists in system

docs.title: "Permission Denied" (Scan Page)
docs.text:
• You're not assigned to this session
  • Contact admin to be added

docs.title: Can't Complete Session (Admin)
docs.text:
• Not all librarians have completed work
  • Check librarian progress on session details page
  • Contact incomplete librarians

docs.text: Action Super Admin Librarian (Assigned) Librarian (Not Assigned) Create Session ✅ ❌ ❌ Start Session ✅ ❌ ❌ Scan Items ✅ ✅ ❌ Complete Work ✅ ✅ ❌ Complete Session ✅ ❌ ❌ Review/Approve ✅ ❌ ❌ View Session ✅ ✅ ✅

docs.title: Email Notifications
docs.text:
Librarians receive emails when:

  • ✉️ Session started (start scanning)
  • ✉️ Session completed (ready for review)
  • ✉️ Session approved (changes applied)
  • ✉️ Session rejected (cancelled)
  • ✉️ Revision requested (need corrections)

Admins receive emails when:

  • ✉️ Librarian completes work
  • ✉️ All librarians complete work (ready to review)

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
For technical issues:

  1. Check this guide
  2. Review session status
  3. Verify permissions
  4. Contact system administrator

Quick Tip: Use the browser's back button to navigate, or use the "Back" links in the UI.

docs.title: Transfer Locations
docs.text: The Transfer Location feature in VOILE allows libraries to manage the movement of items between different branches or nodes. This is essential for multi-branch library systems where materials need to be relocated based on demand, reorganization, or patron requests.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Transfer Location system provides:

  • Request-based workflow - Users request transfers that require approval
  • Node-based routing - Transfers are routed to the target branch for review
  • Approval workflow - Target branch librarians review and approve/deny transfers
  • Audit trail - Complete history of all transfer requests and decisions
  • Real-time item updates - Item locations update immediately upon approval

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
• Quick Reference - Essential commands and workflows for transfers
  • Location Guide - Complete guide for transfer location management

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text: • Flow Diagrams - Visual diagrams of transfer workflows

docs.title: Workflow Overview
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[User Clicks Transfer Location] --> B[Fills Transfer Form]
    B --> C[Selects Target Node & Location]
    C --> D[Provides Reason]
    D --> E[Submits Request]
    E --> F[Status: Pending]
    F --> G{Target Node Librarian Reviews}
    G -->|Approve| H[Item Location Updated]
    G -->|Deny| I[Request Rejected]
    G -->|No Action| F
    H --> J[Status: Approved]
    I --> K[Status: Denied]

docs.title: Transfer States
docs.text: State Description Next Actions Pending ⏳ Awaiting review by target node Review, Delete (by requester) Approved ✅ Transfer completed, item moved View only Denied ❌ Transfer rejected with reason View only Cancelled 🚫 Requester withdrew the request View only

docs.title: As a Requester
docs.text:
1. Navigate to an item in the collection
  2. Click "Transfer Location" button
  3. Select the target node and specific location
  4. Provide a reason for the transfer
  5. Submit and wait for approval

docs.title: As a Reviewer (Target Node Librarian)
docs.text:
1. Check /manage/transfers for pending requests
  2. Review transfer details and reason
  3. Approve or deny with notes
  4. Item location updates automatically upon approval

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
Permission Description transfer_requests.create Can request transfers transfer_requests.read Can view transfers transfer_requests.update Can edit transfers transfer_requests.delete Can delete own pending requests transfer_requests.review Can approve/deny transfers

Default Role Access:

  • super_admin → All permissions
  • librarian → All permissions
  • Other roles → No access by default

docs.title: URL Paths
docs.text:
• View All Transfers: /manage/transfers
  • View Transfer Detail: /manage/transfers/:id

docs.title: For Staff
docs.text:
• Quick transfer initiation from item detail pages
  • Clear status tracking for all requests
  • Ability to cancel pending requests
  • View complete transfer history

docs.title: For Librarians
docs.text:
• Filtered view of transfers for their node
  • Approve/deny with notes
  • Batch review capabilities
  • Audit trail for accountability

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• ✅ Transfers execute immediately upon approval
  • ✅ Item location updates are permanent
  • ✅ Only target node librarians can review
  • ✅ Full audit trail is maintained
  • ⚠️ Cannot undo approved/denied transfers
  • ⚠️ Can only delete your own pending requests

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• Catalog Module Guide - Understanding items and collections
  • Node Loan Rules - Node configuration
  • RBAC Guide - Permission management

docs.title: Flow Diagrams
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant L1 as Librarian A<br/>(Faculty of Science)
    participant UI as Web Interface
    participant BE as Backend/Context
    participant DB as Database
    participant L2 as Librarian B<br/>(Faculty of Arts)

    Note over L1,L2: Transfer Request Creation

    L1->>UI: Clicks "Transfer Location"<br/>on Item X
    UI->>L1: Shows transfer form
    L1->>UI: Fills form:<br/>- To: Faculty of Arts<br/>- Location: Shelf A-12<br/>- Reason: More relevant
    UI->>BE: create_transfer_request()
    BE->>DB: INSERT transfer_request<br/>status: pending
    DB-->>BE: Request created
    BE-->>UI: Success
    UI-->>L1: "Transfer request created"

    Note over L1,L2: Transfer Review Process

    L2->>UI: Opens /manage/transfers
    UI->>BE: list_transfer_requests<br/>(filter: to_node = Arts)
    BE->>DB: SELECT pending transfers
    DB-->>BE: Pending transfers
    BE-->>UI: List of requests
    UI-->>L2: Shows pending requests

    L2->>UI: Clicks on Request
    UI->>BE: get_transfer_request!(id)
    BE->>DB: SELECT transfer details
    DB-->>BE: Full request data
    BE-->>UI: Request details
    UI-->>L2: Shows review interface

    alt Approve Transfer
        L2->>UI: Clicks "Approve"<br/>with optional notes
        UI->>BE: approve_transfer_request()

        activate BE
        BE->>DB: BEGIN TRANSACTION
        BE->>DB: UPDATE transfer_request<br/>status: approved<br/>reviewed_by: L2<br/>reviewed_at: now()
        BE->>DB: UPDATE item<br/>unit_id: Arts<br/>location: Shelf A-12
        BE->>DB: UPDATE transfer_request<br/>completed_at: now()
        BE->>DB: COMMIT TRANSACTION
        deactivate BE

        DB-->>BE: Transfer completed
        BE-->>UI: Success
        UI-->>L2: "Transfer approved"

        Note over L1,DB: Item is now in<br/>Faculty of Arts,<br/>Shelf A-12

    else Deny Transfer
        L2->>UI: Clicks "Deny"<br/>with notes
        UI->>BE: deny_transfer_request()
        BE->>DB: UPDATE transfer_request<br/>status: denied<br/>reviewed_by: L2<br/>reviewed_at: now()<br/>notes: "Reason..."
        DB-->>BE: Status updated
        BE-->>UI: Success
        UI-->>L2: "Transfer denied"

        Note over L1,DB: Item remains in<br/>Faculty of Science
    end

    Note over L1,L2: Both librarians can view<br/>transfer history anytime

docs.title: Transfer States Diagram
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Pending: Request Created

    Pending --> Approved: Librarian Approves
    Pending --> Denied: Librarian Denies
    Pending --> Cancelled: Requester Cancels

    Approved --> [*]: Transfer Complete<br/>(Item Moved)
    Denied --> [*]: Transfer Rejected<br/>(Item Stays)
    Cancelled --> [*]: Request Withdrawn

    note right of Pending
        Can be deleted by requester
        Awaits review
    end note

    note right of Approved
        Item location updated
        Cannot be undone
        Audit trail preserved
    end note

    note right of Denied
        Item unchanged
        Notes explain reason
        Cannot be undone
    end note

docs.title: Authorization Flow
docs.text:
graph TB
    A[User Action] --> B{Has Permission?}

    B -->|No| C[Access Denied]
    B -->|Yes| D{Action Type?}

    D -->|Create| E{Has transfer_requests.create?}
    D -->|View| F{Has transfer_requests.read?}
    D -->|Review| G{Has transfer_requests.review<br/>AND<br/>User's node = target node?}
    D -->|Delete| H{Has transfer_requests.delete<br/>AND<br/>User is requester<br/>AND<br/>Status is pending?}

    E -->|Yes| I[Create Transfer Request]
    E -->|No| C

    F -->|Yes| J[View Transfers]
    F -->|No| C

    G -->|Yes| K[Approve/Deny Transfer]
    G -->|No| C

    H -->|Yes| L[Delete Request]
    H -->|No| C

    I --> M[Success]
    J --> M
    K --> M
    L --> M

    style C fill:#f88
    style M fill:#8f8

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph "Collection Show Page"
        A[Item Card] -->|Click Transfer| B[Transfer Modal]
    end

    subgraph "Transfer Form"
        B --> C[Select Target Node]
        C --> D[Enter Target Location]
        D --> E[Provide Reason]
        E --> F[Submit Request]
    end

    subgraph "Database"
        F -->|Creates| G[TransferRequest<br/>status: pending]
        G -.->|References| H[Item<br/>unchanged]
    end

    subgraph "Transfer List"
        G --> I[Pending Requests View]
        I -->|Filter by| J[Target Node]
        I -->|Filter by| K[Status]
    end

    subgraph "Review Process"
        I -->|Click| L[Transfer Detail View]
        L -->|Approve| M{Execute Transfer}
        L -->|Deny| N{Update Status Only}
    end

    subgraph "Approval"
        M -->|Updates| O[TransferRequest<br/>status: approved]
        M -->|Updates| P[Item<br/>unit_id + location<br/>changed]
        O -.->|Audit Trail| Q[reviewed_by<br/>reviewed_at<br/>completed_at]
    end

    subgraph "Denial"
        N -->|Updates| R[TransferRequest<br/>status: denied]
        N -->|No Change| S[Item<br/>unchanged]
        R -.->|Audit Trail| T[reviewed_by<br/>reviewed_at<br/>notes]
    end

    style A fill:#e1f5ff
    style G fill:#fff3e0
    style O fill:#c8e6c9
    style R fill:#ffcdd2
    style P fill:#c8e6c9
    style S fill:#fff9c4

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Transfer Location feature enables librarians and staff to request and manage the transfer of items between different nodes (locations/faculties) within the system. This feature implements a request-and-approval workflow to ensure proper control over item movements.

docs.title: 1. Database Schema
docs.text: Table: transfer_requests - Stores all transfer requests with their status and details - Tracks from/to locations and nodes - Records who requested and who reviewed the transfer - Maintains audit trail with timestamps

docs.title: 2. Transfer Request States
docs.text:
• Pending: Initial state when a transfer is requested
  • Approved: Transfer has been reviewed and approved; item location is updated
  • Denied: Transfer has been reviewed and rejected
  • Cancelled: Requester cancelled their own pending request

docs.title: Creating a Transfer Request
docs.text:
1. Who can request: Any librarian with transfer_requests.create permission
  2. How to request:
  3. From the collection show page, click "Transfer Location" button on any item card
  4. Fill in the transfer form:
    • Select target node (required)
    • Enter target location (required)
    • Provide reason for transfer (required)

  5. Submit the request

  6. What happens:

  7. A new transfer request is created with status "pending"
  8. The request is recorded with the current item location
  9. No changes are made to the item until approval

docs.title: Reviewing Transfer Requests
docs.text:
1. Who can review: Librarians assigned to the target node with transfer_requests.review permission
  2. Where to review: Navigate to /manage/transfers
  3. Review process:
  4. View pending transfer requests filtered by your node
  5. Click on a request to see full details
  6. Add review notes (optional for approval, recommended for denial)

  7. Choose to approve or deny

  8. What happens on approval:

  9. Transfer request status changes to "approved"
  10. Item's unit_id and location fields are updated immediately
  11. Approval timestamp and reviewer are recorded

  12. Completion timestamp is set

  13. What happens on denial:

  14. Transfer request status changes to "denied"
  15. Item location remains unchanged
  16. Review notes and timestamp are recorded

docs.title: Managing Transfer Requests
docs.text:
List View (/manage/transfers) - View all transfer requests - Filter by: - Status (pending, approved, denied, cancelled) - Target node - Default view shows pending requests for your node - See requester, item details, and dates

Detail View (/manage/transfers/:id) - Full request information - Item details - Transfer from/to information - Request and review history - Review interface (if you can review)

docs.title: Deleting Transfer Requests
docs.text:
• Only the requester can delete their own pending requests
  • Approved, denied, or cancelled requests cannot be deleted (audit trail)

docs.title: Required Permissions
docs.text:
1. transfer_requests.create - Create new transfer requests
  2. transfer_requests.read - View transfer requests
  3. transfer_requests.update - Update transfer requests
  4. transfer_requests.delete - Delete own pending requests
  5. transfer_requests.review - Approve or deny transfer requests

docs.title: Role Assignments
docs.text:
• super_admin: All transfer permissions
  • librarian: All transfer permissions (can request and review)
  • Other roles: No transfer permissions by default

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
defmodule Voile.Schema.Catalog.TransferRequest do
  # Fields:
  # - item_id: Item being transferred
  # - from_node_id: Current node
  # - to_node_id: Target node
  # - from_location: Current location description
  # - to_location: Target location description
  # - status: pending/approved/denied/cancelled
  # - reason: Why transfer is needed
  # - notes: Review notes
  # - requested_by_id: Who requested
  # - reviewed_by_id: Who reviewed
  # - reviewed_at: When reviewed
  # - completed_at: When transfer completed
end

docs.title: Context Functions
docs.text: Voile.Schema.Catalog - list_transfer_requests/1 - List with filtering - get_transfer_request!/1 - Get single request - create_transfer_request/1 - Create new request - update_transfer_request/2 - Update request - approve_transfer_request/2 - Approve and execute transfer - deny_transfer_request/3 - Deny transfer - cancel_transfer_request/1 - Cancel request - list_pending_transfers_for_node/1 - Get pending for node - list_transfer_history_for_item/1 - Get item's history

docs.title: LiveView Modules
docs.text:
1. TransferRequestLive.Index - List and filter transfers
  2. TransferRequestLive.Show - View and review transfers
  3. TransferRequestLive.FormComponent - Create transfer requests

docs.title: As a Requester (Faculty of Science Librarian)
docs.text:
1. You notice an item should be moved to Faculty of Arts
  2. Navigate to the collection containing the item
  3. Click "Transfer Location" on the item card
  4. Fill in the form:
  5. Target Node: Faculty of Arts
  6. Target Location: "Shelf A-12, Reading Room"
  7. Reason: "This item is more relevant to Arts curriculum"
  8. Submit request
  9. Wait for Faculty of Arts librarian to review

docs.title: As a Reviewer (Faculty of Arts Librarian)
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Transfer Requests page (/manage/transfers)
  2. See pending requests for your faculty (auto-filtered)
  3. Click on a request to review details
  4. Check if the transfer makes sense for your collection
  5. Add notes if needed: "Accepted. We have space in the Arts reading room."
  6. Click "Approve Transfer"
  7. Item is now in your faculty's inventory

docs.title: Checking Transfer History
docs.text:
1. Navigate to an item's detail page
  2. Use Catalog.list_transfer_history_for_item(item_id) in code
  3. See all past transfer requests for this item

docs.title: Future Enhancements
docs.text:
Potential improvements to consider:

  1. Notifications: Email notifications to reviewers when new requests arrive
  2. Bulk Transfers: Transfer multiple items at once
  3. Transfer Templates: Save common transfer destinations
  4. Scheduled Transfers: Set future date for transfer execution
  5. Transfer Reports: Analytics on transfer patterns
  6. Physical Transfer: Track physical item movement with checkpoints
  7. Transfer Notes: Allow adding tracking notes during physical transfer

docs.title: "You cannot delete this transfer request"
docs.text: • Only pending requests can be deleted, and only by the requester

docs.title: "You are not authorized to review this transfer"
docs.text: • Only librarians assigned to the target node can review transfers to their node

docs.title: Transfer request created but item not moved
docs.text:
• Transfer doesn't execute until approved
  • Check if request is still pending

docs.title: Cannot see transfer requests
docs.text:
• Check you have transfer_requests.read permission
  • Check your node assignment matches the transfer target

docs.title: Database Migration
docs.text:
Run the migration to create the transfer_requests table:

mix ecto.migrate

docs.title: Seeding Permissions
docs.text:
To add transfer permissions to your database:

mix run priv/repo/seeds/authorization_seeds.ex

This will create the transfer permissions and assign them to appropriate roles.

docs.title: 🎯 Quick Access
docs.text: Action URL Permission Required View all transfers /manage/transfers transfer_requests.read View transfer detail /manage/transfers/:id transfer_requests.read Create transfer Click "Transfer Location" on item card transfer_requests.create

docs.title: As a Requester
docs.text:
• Click "Transfer Location" button on any item
  • Fill out: target node, location, reason
  • Submit and wait for approval
  • Can delete your own pending requests

docs.title: As a Reviewer (Target Node Librarian)
docs.text:
• Check /manage/transfers for pending requests
  • See requests filtered for your node by default
  • Review details and approve/deny
  • Add notes explaining your decision

docs.title: 📋 Transfer States
docs.text: State Description Next Actions Pending ⏳ Awaiting review Can be reviewed or deleted by requester Approved ✅ Transfer completed Item moved, cannot be undone Denied ❌ Transfer rejected Item unchanged, cannot be undone Cancelled 🚫 Requester withdrew No action taken

docs.title: 🔑 Permissions Quick Guide
docs.text:
transfer_requests.create  → Can request transfers
transfer_requests.read    → Can view transfers
transfer_requests.update  → Can edit transfers
transfer_requests.delete  → Can delete own pending requests
transfer_requests.review  → Can approve/deny transfers

Default Role Access: - super_admin → All permissions - librarian → All permissions - Other roles → No access (can be granted)

docs.title: Request a Transfer
docs.text:
1. Navigate to collection show page
  2. Find item → Click "Transfer Location"
  3. Select target node and location
  4. Write reason → Submit

docs.title: Review a Transfer
docs.text:
1. Go to /manage/transfers
  2. Click on pending request
  3. Review details
  4. Add notes (optional)
  5. Click "Approve" or "Deny"

docs.title: Check Transfer History
docs.text:
• View item's past transfers in the system
  • Use filters to find specific transfers
  • Search by item, node, or status

docs.title: Cancel Your Request
docs.text:
1. Go to /manage/transfers
  2. Find your pending request
  3. Click "Delete"
  4. Confirm deletion

docs.title: 🎨 Status Badge Colors
docs.text:
• 🟡 Pending - Yellow badge
  • 🟢 Approved - Green badge
  • 🔴 Denied - Red badge
  • ⚪ Cancelled - Gray badge

docs.title: 🔍 Filters Available
docs.text:
By Status: - All Statuses - Pending - Approved - Denied - Cancelled

By Node: - All Nodes - [Your Nodes]

docs.title: ⚠️ Important Notes
docs.text:
• ✅ Transfers execute immediately upon approval
  • ✅ Item location updates are permanent
  • ✅ Only target node librarians can review
  • ✅ Full audit trail is maintained
  • ⚠️ Cannot undo approved/denied transfers
  • ⚠️ Can only delete your own pending requests

docs.title: 🐛 Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Problem: Cannot see transfer button - Check: You need transfer_requests.create permission

Problem: Cannot review transfer - Check: Your node must match the target node - Check: You need transfer_requests.review permission

Problem: Cannot delete transfer request - Check: Status must be "pending" - Check: You must be the requester

Problem: Item not moved after approval - Check: Transfer status shows "approved" - Check: Completion timestamp is set - Verify: Item location was updated in database

docs.title: 📞 Need Help?
docs.text: See full documentation:

docs.title: Visitor Management System
docs.text: This document describes the Visitor Management System feature, which allows tracking visitors, collecting feedback through surveys, and generating reports.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Visitor Management System consists of three main components:

  1. Public Visitor Check-In - A public-facing page where visitors can register their visits
  2. Public Survey Form - A public-facing page where visitors can provide feedback
  3. Dashboard & Reports - Staff/admin pages for viewing statistics

The system uses existing mst_locations (master locations) data, so there's no need for separate room management - simply configure your locations through the existing master data management interface.

Note: The visitor system was refactored to use the existing mst_locations table. The migration 20260204162521_refactor_visitor_to_use_locations.exs renames visitor_room_id to location_id and removes the visitor_rooms table. After updating your code, run mix ecto.migrate and ensure mst_locations entries have is_active = true.

docs.title: visitor_logs
docs.text:
Records each visitor check-in.

  • id - Primary key
  • visitor_identifier - User ID, student number, or name (required)
  • location_id - Foreign key to mst_locations (required)

Indexes: - location_id - node_id - ip_address - IP address - user_agent - Browser user agent - additional_data - JSONB field for extra data - visitor_log_id - Optional link to visitor log - location_id - Foreign key to mst_locations (required) - node_id - Foreign key to nodes (required) - inserted_at, updated_at - Timestamps Indexes: 2. Select location within the node (from mst_locations) - node_id - visitor_log_id - rating - inserted_at - survey_type

docs.title: Visitor Check-In
docs.text:
1. Select location within the node (from mst_locations)

Access: Public (no authentication required)

docs.title: Visitor Log Entry
docs.text:
• location_id - Foreign key to mst_locations (required) Features:
  • Multi-step workflow:

docs.title: Survey Entry
docs.text:
• location_id - Foreign key to mst_locations (required)
  • Select room/location within the node (from mst_locations)
  • Fill in visitor information
  • Virtual keyboard for easy data entry
  • Input fields:
  • Visitor identifier (ID, student number, or name) - Required
  • Visitor origin/type (Student, Faculty, Alumni, etc.) - Optional
  • Auto-reset after successful check-in

docs.title: Visitor Survey
docs.text:
URL: /visitor/survey

Access: Public (no authentication required)

Features: - Multi-step workflow: 1. Select location/node 2. Select room/location within the node (from mst_locations) 3. Submit feedback - Star-based rating (1-5 stars) - Required - Optional text comment (up to 2000 characters) - Auto-reset after successful submission

docs.title: Visitor Statistics Dashboard
docs.text:
URL: /manage/visitor/statistics

Access: Staff and Admin users (requires authentication)

Features: - Specific room/location - Statistics shown: - Visitors by room - Visitors by origin/type - Rating distribution - Daily visitor trend - Real-time refresh button

docs.title: Location Management
docs.text:
The visitor system uses the existing mst_locations table for room/location data. To manage visitor locations:

  1. Navigate to the master locations management interface
  2. Ensure locations are assigned to the correct node
  3. Set is_active to true for locations that should appear in visitor check-in
  4. Use location_name as the display name for visitors

Note: There is no separate visitor room management interface - all location data is managed through the existing master data system.

docs.title: Visitor Logs
docs.text:
# List visitor logs with filters
System.list_visitor_logs(opts \\ [])
# Options: node_id, location_id, from_date, to_date, search, preload, limit

# Get a single visitor log
System.get_visitor_log!(id, opts \\ [])

# Create a visitor log (check-in)
System.create_visitor_log(attrs)

# Update a visitor log (e.g., check-out)
System.update_visitor_log(visitor_log, attrs)

# Delete a visitor log
System.delete_visitor_log(visitor_log)

docs.title: Visitor Surveys
docs.text:
System.update_visitor_survey(visitor_survey, attrs)

# Delete a visitor survey
System.delete_visitor_survey(visitor_survey)

docs.title: Get comprehensive visitor statistics
docs.text: System.get_visitor_statistics(opts \ [])

docs.title: by_room: [%{room_id: id, room_name: name, count: count}],

docs.text:
## Setup Instructions

1. **Run Migrations**
   ```bash
   mix ecto.migrate
   ```

2. **Configure Locations** (use existing master data)
   - Ensure your `mst_locations` table has locations configured
   - Assign each location to the appropriate `node_id`
   - Set `is_active = true` for locations that should appear in visitor check-in
   - The `location_name` field will be displayed to visitors

3. **Verify Locations**
   - Check that each node has at least one active location
   - Locations will be displayed in alphabetical order by `location_name`

4. **Test the Public Pages**
   - Navigate to `/visitor` to test check-in
   - Navigate to `/visitor/survey` to test survey submission

6. **View Statistics**
   - Go to `/manage/visitor/statistics`
   - Filter by date range, location, or room
   - Export or analyze the data as needed

## Virtual Keyboard

The visitor check-in page includes a built-in virtual keyboard for easy data entry, especially useful for:
- Kiosk deployments
- Touch screen interfaces
- Environments where physical keyboards are not available

**Features:**
- QWERTY layout
- Numbers (0-9)
- Special characters (@, -, _, .)
- Backspace and Clear functions
- Alternative numeric-only layout available

**Usage:**
```elixir
<VirtualKeyboard.virtual_keyboard target="input_field_id" layout="qwerty" />
# Or for numeric only:
<VirtualKeyboard.virtual_keyboard target="input_field_id" layout="numeric" />

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Location Naming
  2. Use clear, descriptive location_name values
  3. Keep names short for mobile displays

  4. Be consistent across nodes

  5. Data Privacy

  6. The system logs IP addresses for analytics
  7. Consider your privacy policy regarding visitor data

  8. Regularly archive or purge old visitor logs based on retention policy

  9. Survey Design

  10. Keep the survey simple (1-5 stars + optional comment)
  11. Don't make too many fields required

  12. The system is designed for quick feedback

  13. Statistics

  14. Use date filters to analyze trends
  15. Compare different locations

  16. Look at rating distributions to identify areas for improvement

  17. Maintenance

  18. Regularly review active locations in mst_locations
  19. Set is_active = false for locations that are no longer in use
  20. Update location descriptions as needed through master data management

docs.title: Integration with Existing Systems
docs.text:
The visitor management system integrates with:

  • Nodes System - Multi-location support
  • Master Locations (mst_locations) - Uses existing location data for visitor check-in
  • User Authentication - For staff/admin access to reports

docs.title: Future Enhancements
docs.text:
Potential features for future development:

  • Export statistics to CSV/Excel
  • Email notifications for low ratings
  • Visitor check-out tracking
  • QR code generation for quick check-in
  • Integration with membership system
  • SMS notifications
  • Visitor badges/receipts
  • Customizable survey questions
  • API endpoints for external integrations
  • Dashboard widgets for homepage
  • Bulk import/export of visitor data

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Problem: Locations not appearing in the check-in list - Ensure the location is_active is set to true in mst_locations - Verify the node_id is correct - Check that the location exists in the database

Problem: Statistics not updating - Click the "Refresh" button - Check the date range filter - Verify data exists for the selected filters

Problem: Virtual keyboard not working - Check browser console for JavaScript errors - Ensure Phoenix LiveView is properly connected - Verify the target attribute matches the input field ID

docs.title: Support
docs.text: For questions or issues related to the Visitor Management System, please contact the development team or file an issue in the project repository.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Visitor Management System allows you to track visitors, collect feedback, and generate comprehensive reports. It's designed for libraries, museums, and other public institutions that need to monitor visitor traffic and satisfaction.

docs.title: 1. Run Migrations
docs.text:
cd voile
mix ecto.migrate

This creates two new tables:

Note: the Visitor Management system was recently refactored to use the existing mst_locations master data instead of a separate visitor_rooms table. See the "Refactoring notes" section below for important migration and API changes.

The system uses your existing mst_locations table for room/location data.

docs.title: 2. Configure Locations (Use Existing Master Data)
docs.text:
Ensure your mst_locations table has locations configured:

No additional seeding is needed - the system uses your existing location data.

docs.title: Manage Locations
docs.text: Use your existing master data management interface to manage locations that appear in the visitor system.

docs.title: 🎯 Public Visitor Check-In
docs.text:
1. Select your location (node)
  2. Choose the room you're visiting
  3. Enter your ID/name with built-in virtual keyboard

docs.title: ⭐ Visitor Surveys
docs.text:
• 5-star rating system (required)

  • Optional comment field (up to 2000 characters)

  • Select location

  • Select room
  • Submit rating and feedback

docs.title: 📊 Statistics Dashboard (Staff/Admin)
docs.text:
• Date range
  • Location/Node

  • Specific room

  • Total visitors

  • Unique visitors
  • Average survey rating
  • Visitors by room
  • Visitors by origin/type
  • Rating distribution
  • Daily visitor trends

docs.title: Configure a Location for Visitors
docs.text:
1. Go to your master locations management interface
  2. Find or create a location in mst_locations
  3. Set these fields:
  4. node_id: The library branch/node
  5. location_name: Name shown to visitors (e.g., "Main Lobby")
  6. description: Optional description
  7. is_active: Set to true to make it visible to visitors
  8. Save the location

docs.title: View Today's Visitors
docs.text:
1. Go to /manage/visitor/statistics
  2. Set date range to today
  3. Optionally filter by location or room
  4. View the statistics and charts

docs.title: Check In a Visitor (Kiosk Mode)
docs.text:
1. Navigate to /visitor on a tablet/kiosk
  2. Tap your location
  3. Tap the room you're visiting
  4. Use the virtual keyboard to enter your ID or name
  5. (Optional) Select where you're from
  6. Tap "Check In"

docs.title: Common Visitor Origins
docs.text:
The system includes these default visitor types:

You can use these to segment your visitor data in reports.

docs.title: Visitor Log Entry
docs.text:
%{
  visitor_identifier: "12345" or "John Doe",
  visitor_origin: "Student",
  location_id: 1,
  node_id: 1,
  check_in_time: ~U[2024-02-04 12:30:00Z],
  ip_address: "192.168.1.100",
  user_agent: "Mozilla/5.0..."
}

docs.title: Survey Entry
docs.text:
%{
  rating: 5,
  comment: "Great facilities and helpful staff!",
  location_id: 1,
  node_id: 1,
  survey_type: "general"
}

docs.title: For Kiosk Deployments
docs.text:
1. Use a tablet in landscape mode
  2. Enable kiosk mode in your browser to prevent navigation
  3. Mount the tablet at an accessible height
  4. Consider adding clear signage directing visitors to check in
  5. Use the virtual keyboard for easier touch input

docs.title: For Data Analysis
docs.text:
1. Review statistics weekly to identify trends
  2. Monitor average ratings to gauge satisfaction
  3. Check visitor distribution across rooms for space planning
  4. Read comments regularly to identify improvement areas
  5. Export data periodically for long-term analysis

docs.title: For Room Setup
docs.text:
1. Name rooms clearly and consistently
  2. Set logical display orders (entrance → interior spaces)
  3. Keep active rooms relevant (deactivate unused spaces)
  4. Update descriptions seasonally if needed
  5. Group similar rooms across nodes for consistency

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Q: I don't see any locations when trying to check in

Q: Statistics showing zero visitors

Q: Virtual keyboard not responding

Q: Can't manage visitor locations

docs.title: API Usage (Future)
docs.text:
While not yet implemented, future versions will support:

GET /api/v1/visitor/statistics?from=2024-01-01&to=2024-01-31&node_id=1

  "visitor_identifier": "12345",

# Submit survey via API

  "rating": 5,
  "comment": "Excellent service!",
  "location_id": 1
}

docs.title: Refactoring notes
docs.text:
• Migration file: priv/repo/migrations/20260204162521_refactor_visitor_to_use_locations.exs
  • What changed:
  • visitor_room_id columns were renamed to location_id in visitor_logs and visitor_surveys
  • The visitor_rooms table and associated schema/UI were removed
  • LiveViews and context functions now use Master.list_locations(node_id: ..., is_active: true)
  • After pulling latest code, run the migration:
mix ecto.migrate
  • Verify locations in mst_locations have node_id, location_name, and is_active = true.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to VOILE! This section will guide you through setting up and configuring your development environment.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, make sure you have the following installed:

  • Elixir v1.18.0 or later
  • Erlang/OTP 27.1 or later
  • PostgreSQL 14 or newer
  • Node.js (for asset compilation)

docs.title: 1. Environment Setup
docs.text:
Set up your development environment with the correct configuration files and environment variables.

Read the Environment Setup Guide →

docs.title: 2. Admin User Setup
docs.text:
Create your first administrator account and understand the default credentials.

Read the Admin User Guide →

docs.title: 3. Seeds Setup
docs.text:
Populate the database with initial data including permissions, roles, and sample content.

Read the Seeds Setup Guide →

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
For the fastest setup, run these commands:

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/chrisnaadhi/voile.git
cd voile

# Install dependencies
mix deps.get

# Setup database (creates, migrates, and seeds)
mix ecto.setup

# Install Node.js dependencies and build assets
mix assets.setup
mix assets.build

# Start the Phoenix server
mix phx.server

Then visit http://localhost:4000 in your browser.

docs.title: Default Login Credentials
docs.text:
After running seeds, you can log in with:

  • Email: admin@voile.id
  • Password: super_long_password

Warning

Change the default password immediately after your first login!

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
After completing the setup:

  1. Explore the Architecture to understand the system design
  2. Review the Catalog Module to manage collections
  3. Configure Authentication for proper access control

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
If you encounter issues during setup:

  1. Ensure all prerequisites are installed correctly
  2. Check that PostgreSQL is running and accessible
  3. Verify your environment variables are set correctly
  4. Review the terminal output for specific error messages

For more help, check the Reference section.

docs.title: Default Admin User
docs.text: After running mix ecto.reset, you'll have a default admin user:

docs.title: Login Credentials
docs.text:
• Email: admin@voile.id
  • Username: admin
  • Password: super_long_password

docs.title: Assigned Permissions
docs.text:
• Role: super_admin (27 permissions)
  • Member Type: Administrator

docs.title: ⚠️ Security Warning
docs.text:
CHANGE THE PASSWORD IMMEDIATELY after first login!

The default password is for development purposes only and should NEVER be used in production.

docs.title: What Happens During Seeding
docs.text:
1. Permissions Created (27 total)

  2. Collections, Items, Metadata, Users, Roles, System

  3. Roles Created (5 total)

  4. super_admin, admin, editor, contributor, viewer

  5. Member Types Created (6 total)

  6. Administrator, Staff, Member (Org), Member (Verified), Member (Affirmation), Guest

  7. Admin User Created

  8. Email: admin@voile.id
  9. Automatically assigned super_admin role
  10. Member type: Administrator

docs.title: Verify Setup
docs.text:
After seeding, verify in IEx:

# Start IEx
iex -S mix

# Check admin user exists
alias Voile.Repo
alias Voile.Schema.Accounts.User
alias VoileWeb.Auth.Authorization

user = Repo.get_by(User, email: "admin@voile.id")
user = Repo.preload(user, [:user_role_assignments, :roles])

# Should show super_admin role
IO.inspect(user.roles, label: "Admin Roles")

# Check permissions
Authorization.can?(user, "system.settings")
# => true

Authorization.can?(user, "collections.delete")
# => true

docs.title: Admin user created but no role assigned
docs.text:
If you see this message: 

⚠️  super_admin role not found. Please run authorization seeds first.

Solution: The authorization seeds must run BEFORE master.exs. This is already configured in seeds.exs.

docs.title: Role already assigned
docs.text:
If you see: 

ℹ️  Admin user already has super_admin role

This is normal when running seeds multiple times. The seeds are idempotent.

docs.title: Adding More Admins
docs.text:
To create additional admin users with super_admin role:

# In IEx
alias Voile.Repo
alias Voile.Schema.Accounts.{User, Role}
alias VoileWeb.Auth.Authorization

# Create new user
{:ok, new_admin} = %User{}
|> User.registration_changeset(%{
  email: "newadmin@example.com",
  username: "newadmin",
  fullname: "New Admin",
  password: "another_super_long_password",
  user_type_id: admin_member_type_id,
  node_id: 20
}, hash_password: true)
|> Repo.insert()

# Assign super_admin role
super_admin = Repo.get_by(Role, name: "super_admin")
{:ok, _} = Authorization.assign_role(new_admin.id, super_admin.id)

  • Master seeds: priv/repo/seeds/master.exs
